**T6.6 · Shared Verse Anchors**  (21)

- `T6.6.1` · cluster_finding · /cluster_synthesis — **[CLUSTER]** E — The shared verse analysis reveals the cluster's key structural nexus points. *Mat11:29* (CHAR-1 primary, CHAR-4 secondary): the verse that most directly confirms CHAR-1 and CHAR-4 as co-constitutive of the same inner posture in their expression through Jesus's self-description. *Phili2:8* (CHAR-1 + CHAR-2 jointly): the paradigmatic verse holding both the humbling and the obedience in one act — the most important cluster-internal shared verse for establishing the CHAR-1/CHAR-2 relationship. *Isa57:15* (CHAR-3 anchor, CHAR-1 boundary-marker): the verse that simultaneously defines CHAR-3 and marks its boundary with CHAR-1 (contrite and lowly in spirit — two characteristics named side-by-side). *1Ti3:4* (CHAR-5 and CHAR-2 joint vc_ids): the single verse-record most directly evidencing the CHAR-5 → CHAR-2 causal relationship (the overseer's dignity producing the children's submission). *Psa51:12* (exclusively CHAR-6): the cluster's most isolated anchor — no other M09 characteristic shares this verse, confirming willing-heartedness's distinctive position as terminologically and analytically separate from the other five. Cluster-level: the cluster's key structural verses are Mat11:29 (CHAR-1/CHAR-4 nexus), Phili2:8 (CHAR-1/CHAR-2 Christological centre), Isa57:15 (CHAR-1/CHAR-3 boundary), 1Ti3:4 (CHAR-5/CHAR-2 causal), and Psa51:12 (CHAR-6 exclusive).  ---
- `T6.6.1` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-1]** E — Mat11:29 (vc_id 21101) is noted as MULTI-FACETED at Phase 7: primary anchor for M09-A-VCG-04 (Humility — relational posture) and secondary member of M09-F-VCG-01 (Meekness — calibrated restraint and gentleness). The verse therefore contributes to both CHAR-1 Humility and CHAR-4 Meekness/Gentleness. Phili2:8 (vc_id 21117), anchor of M09-A-VCG-03, is the paradigmatic Christological humility verse — it also functions within the broader Phili2 Submission context, creating a shared anchor space between CHAR-1 and CHAR-2. 1Pe5:5 carries two vc_ids (21108 tapeinos, 21115 tapeinofrosunē), both in M09-A-VCG-04 — a shared verse within CHAR-1 rather than across characteristics.
- `T6.6.1` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-2]** E — Heb5:8 (vc_id 30255, anchor of M09-C-VCG-04) also carries weight in the CHAR-1 Humility study — the verse was cited extensively in CHAR-1's T5 and T6 as the paradigmatic evidence for submission forming through suffering. Phili2:8 (outside this specific corpus but the immediate context of Phili2:12 in this corpus) is the primary anchor of M09-A-VCG-03 (CHAR-1 Humility's apostolic-vocation VCG). Rom6:17 (M09-C-VCG-01 anchor) is distinctive to CHAR-2. 1Pe1:22 (M09-C-VCG-04) is the most directly soul-level submission verse and may inform future cluster work on soul-purification characteristics. Within the programme's current clusters, 1Pe1:2 (M09-D-VCG-02) is relevant to Spirit and sanctification characteristics.
- `T6.6.1` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-3]** E — Isa57:15 (vc_id 62969, anchor of M09-E-VCG-01) was cited in CHAR-1 Humility's Phase 9 study (T6.1.1 and T6.5.2) as the verse that provides the precise boundary between contrition and lowliness — its pairing of "contrite and lowly in spirit" makes it the most precise boundary-marker verse for the CHAR-1/CHAR-3 distinction. The verse thus serves as an anchor in CHAR-3 and as a boundary-defining reference in CHAR-1, without being formally cited as a CHAR-1 verse (it is exclusively in §3.B evidence).
- `T6.6.1` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-4]** E — Act9:38 (vc_id 60786) and Heb5:2 (vc_id 6225) are specific to M09-F and do not appear as anchors in other M09 characteristic VCGs. The multi-faceted note designates Mat11:29 (vc_id 21101) as secondary to M09-F — that verse is the primary anchor of M09-A-VCG-04 (CHAR-1) and carries the secondary gentleness contribution to M09-F. However, Mat11:29 is not in §3.C and cannot be claimed as CHAR-4's own anchor.
- `T6.6.1` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-5]** E — 1Ti3:4 (vc_id 12082) is shared with M09-D-VCG-03 in CHAR-2 Submission: the semnotēs vc_id routes to M09-G-VCG-01 (CHAR-5); the hupotagē vc_id (vc_id 38461) of the same verse_record routes to M09-D-VCG-03 (CHAR-2). The same verse carries both CHAR-5 (the overseer's dignity) and CHAR-2 (his children's submission) — two vc_ids, two characteristics, one verse_record. This shared verse is analytically significant: it is the verse that most directly evidences the CHAR-5 → CHAR-2 causal relationship (T6.2.1 — dignity producing submission).
- `T6.6.1` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-6]** E — Psa51:12 (vc_id 43494, anchor of M09-H-VCG-02) is the primary anchor of CHAR-6's VCG-02. This verse is analytically distinctive within M09 as the only verse that: (a) explicitly locates the characteristic in the spirit (ruach); (b) names it as a divine gift requiring seeking; and (c) pairs it with joy of salvation. No other M09 characteristic has a primary anchor in Psa51:12. The verse is exclusively CHAR-6's within this programme, not shared with other characteristics in M09.
- `T6.6.2` · cluster_finding · /cluster_synthesis — **[CLUSTER]** E — The shared verse anchors reveal specific structural relationships within the cluster. Mat11:29 (multi-faceted — CHAR-1 primary; CHAR-4 secondary): reveals that humility and meekness/gentleness are structurally inseparable in Jesus's self-description — the paradigmatic expression of both characteristics occurs in the same self-designation; CHAR-4 is the relational-engagement expression of what CHAR-1 names as inner posture. Phili2:8 (CHAR-1 + CHAR-2 jointly): reveals that humility and submission are Christologically co-constitutive — the Son's single kenotic act simultaneously expresses both; neither alone describes the full reality of the act. Isa57:15 (CHAR-3 anchor; CHAR-1 boundary-marker): reveals CHAR-1 and CHAR-3 as permanently adjacent and mutually defining — the verse that most directly defines their co-occurrence and their distinction. 1Ti3:4 (CHAR-5 and CHAR-2 shared verse_record): reveals the CHAR-5 → CHAR-2 causal relationship as directly verse-evidenced — the only verse in the cluster that holds the cause (overseer's dignity) and the effect (children's submission) in a single statement. Psa51:12 (exclusively CHAR-6): its exclusivity confirms CHAR-6's analytical distinctiveness within M09 — no other characteristic shares this verse; the willing-heartedness is terminologically and analytically self-contained within its cluster.  ---
- `T6.6.2` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-1]** E — Mat11:29's dual-function (Humility primary / Meekness secondary) reveals that the two characteristics are *structurally adjacent and mutually defining*: Jesus's self-description names both in the same breath, implying they operate together in the same inner-being posture. The gentle-and-lowly-in-heart of Mat11:29 is not the addition of two independent characteristics but a single integrated self-description in which humility and meekness are the two faces of the same inner posture. The Phili2 shared space (Humility v.3 / Submission v.8) reveals that humility and submission are *sequentially constitutive*: the call to humility in the community (v.3) finds its paradigm in Christ's obedient self-lowering (v.8), making submission the embodied expression of humility's inner posture.
- `T6.6.2` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-2]** E — The Heb5:8 shared presence (CHAR-2 and CHAR-1) confirms the Phili2 observation: humility and submission are most clearly seen as co-present in the Christological paradigm. The Son who learned obedience through suffering (CHAR-2 — Heb5:8) is the same Son who humbled himself by becoming obedient (CHAR-1 + CHAR-2 — Phili2:8). The characteristics are not merely conceptually adjacent but Christologically co-constitutive: genuine submission requires the humility posture; genuine humility expresses itself in the submission of the will. The shared Christological anchor evidences them as two faces of a single kenotic movement in Christ.
- `T6.6.2` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-3]** E — Isa57:15's significance for both CHAR-1 and CHAR-3 confirms that the verse is the most direct scriptural expression of the CHAR-1/CHAR-3 relationship: it holds both characteristics together in one phrase, defining their co-occurrence and distinguishing them by naming them separately. The verse is thus the primary Christological and analytical nexus of the two characteristics — the single verse that most clearly shows what they share (the lowly-before-God orientation) and how they differ (contrite as the acute form alongside the stable lowly posture).
- `T6.6.2` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-4]** E — The Mat11:29 multi-faceted designation (primary CHAR-1, secondary CHAR-4) confirms the structural proximity of the two characteristics: the single verse carries both humility ("lowly in heart") and meekness/gentleness ("gentle") as co-present qualities. The shared verse evidences that in Jesus's self-description, humility and gentleness are inseparable — the paradigmatic expression of both characteristics occurs in the same self-description, positioning CHAR-4 as the relational expression of what CHAR-1 names as inner posture.
- `T6.6.2` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-5]** E — 1Ti3:4's shared verse_record (CHAR-5 semnotēs and CHAR-2 hupotagē) reveals that dignity and submission are constitutively related in the household authority context: the overseer's dignity (CHAR-5) and the children's submission (CHAR-2) are named in the same sentence as cause and effect. The shared verse evidences that CHAR-5 is the inner character quality that produces CHAR-2 in the authority relationship — dignity is the upstream cause; submission is the downstream ordered effect. No other verse in the programme's evidence clusters the two characteristics in this causal relationship as directly.
- `T6.6.2` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-6]** E — Since Psa51:12 is exclusively CHAR-6's anchor within M09, the absence of shared anchor with other characteristics confirms that willing-heartedness is terminologically and analytically distinct within M09 — it does not have a shared verse-anchor with CHAR-1 Humility, CHAR-2 Submission, CHAR-3 Contrition, CHAR-4 Meekness, or CHAR-5 Dignity. The characteristic stands analytically distinct within the cluster with its own unique verse anchor.
- `T6.6.3` · cluster_finding · /cluster_synthesis — **[CLUSTER]** E — Shared verse anchors are identified and their relational implications analysed (T6.6.1 and T6.6.2). The cluster's key structural verses are: Mat11:29 (CHAR-1/CHAR-4 nexus), Phili2:8 (CHAR-1/CHAR-2 Christological centre), Isa57:15 (CHAR-1/CHAR-3 boundary and co-definition), 1Ti3:4 (CHAR-5/CHAR-2 causal), and Psa51:12 (CHAR-6 exclusive anchor). These five verses are M09's most structurally important for understanding the cluster's internal architecture.  ---
- `T6.6.3` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-1]** E — Shared verse anchors are identified and their relational implications analysed (see T6.6.1 and T6.6.2).
- `T6.6.3` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-2]** E — Shared verse anchors are identified and their implications analysed (see T6.6.1 and T6.6.2).
- `T6.6.3` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-3]** E — Shared verse significance is identified and analysed (see T6.6.1 and T6.6.2).
- `T6.6.3` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-4]** E — Shared verse usage (Mat11:29 multi-faceted) is identified and analysed (see T6.6.1 and T6.6.2). Within §3.C's primary evidence, Act9:38 and Heb5:2 are not shared with other characteristic studies.
- `T6.6.3` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-5]** E — Shared verse significance (1Ti3:4) is identified and analysed (see T6.6.1 and T6.6.2). The shared verse is the direct causal evidence for the CHAR-5 → CHAR-2 relationship.
- `T6.6.3` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-6]** E — No shared verse anchors are identified within M09. Psa51:12 is exclusively CHAR-6's within this cluster (see T6.6.1 and T6.6.2).

**T6.7 · Dimensional Sharing**  (21)

- `T6.7.1` · cluster_finding · /cluster_synthesis — **[CLUSTER]** E — The dimensional sharing analysis reveals M09's internal structure with precision. *Most dimensionally proximate pair*: CHAR-1 and CHAR-2 — four shared dimensions (volitional, relational, moral-evaluative, affective). This is the cluster's most closely related pair, confirmed by Phili2:8 where both are simultaneously exercised. *Second-most proximate pair*: CHAR-1 and CHAR-4 — three shared dimensions (volitional, cognitive, relational), confirmed by Mat11:29. *Deepest constitutional level pair*: CHAR-3 and CHAR-6 — both explicitly spirit- and heart-located (the only two characteristics with explicit spirit-location), making them the cluster's deepest-constitutional-level pair despite their relatively weak surface similarity. *Most dimensionally isolated*: CHAR-5 Dignity — the characterological-primary classification is not shared with any other M09 characteristic; it is the most distinctive and least dimensionally proximate member of the cluster. Cluster-level: the dimensional analysis reveals a cluster with a clear centre (CHAR-1 — dimensionally most proximate to the most other members) and a clear periphery (CHAR-5 — most distinctively characterological, least dimensionally adjacent to neighbours). CHAR-3 and CHAR-6's shared spirit/heart constitutional depth makes them an unexpected pair at the dimensional level despite their surface dissimilarity.  ---  ## T7 — Lexical and Scientific Synthesis
- `T6.7.1` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-1]** E — Based on the evidence accumulated across the tiers: confirmed primary dimensions of CHAR-1 Humility are *volitional-dispositional* (primary), *cognitive*, *affective*, *relational*, and *moral-evaluative*. CHAR-4 Meekness/Gentleness (M09-F) shares the volitional and relational dimensions (calibrated restraint requires volition; meekness is constitutively relational). CHAR-2 Submission (M09-C/D) shares the volitional and relational dimensions and partially the moral-evaluative dimension (conscience engagement — see M09-C evidence). CHAR-3 Contrition (M09-E) shares the affective, moral-evaluative, and conscience dimensions. The dimensional sharing is most extensive with CHAR-2 (Submission) — the two share three confirmed dimensions.
- `T6.7.1` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-2]** E — Confirmed primary dimensions of CHAR-2 Submission: *volitional* (primary), *relational*, *cognitive*, *affective* (reverential fear; love as fruit), *moral-evaluative*, *conscience-grounded*. CHAR-1 Humility shares the volitional, relational, moral-evaluative, and affective dimensions — four dimensions shared. CHAR-3 Contrition (M09-E) shares the affective and conscience dimensions. CHAR-4 Meekness/Gentleness (M09-F) shares the volitional and relational dimensions. The most extensive dimensional sharing is with CHAR-1 (four dimensions), confirming their structural proximity.
- `T6.7.1` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-3]** E — Confirmed primary dimensions of CHAR-3 Contrition: *affective-existential* (primary — the crushed/broken inner state); *relational* (Godward — constitutively relational toward God); *spirit-level constitutional* (explicitly named); *heart-level constitutional* (explicitly named). CHAR-1 Humility shares the relational (Godward) and both constitutional-level dimensions. CHAR-2 Submission shares the relational dimension. The most significant dimensional sharing is with CHAR-1 — three of four confirmed dimensions are shared (relational, spirit-level, heart-level). The primary dimension (affective-existential) is the most distinctive of CHAR-3 — CHAR-1's primary is volitional-dispositional, not affective-existential.
- `T6.7.1` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-4]** E — Confirmed primary dimensions of CHAR-4: *volitional-character* (primary — metriopatheō as will's calibrated quality; okneō as will's kinetic readiness); *affective* (secondary — measured compassion in metriopatheō); *cognitive* (secondary — self-assessment of shared weakness); *relational* (constitutive — both terms are relational in structure). CHAR-1 Humility shares the volitional, cognitive, and relational dimensions — three of four confirmed dimensions. CHAR-2 Submission shares the volitional and relational dimensions. The most extensive dimensional sharing is with CHAR-1 (three dimensions), confirming proximity.
- `T6.7.1` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-5]** E — Confirmed primary dimensions of CHAR-5 Dignity: *characterological* (primary — stable comprehensive character quality); *relational* (social-ordering expression); *moral-evaluative* (integrity pairing); *volitional* (secondary — maintained through volitional commitment). CHAR-1 Humility shares the relational and moral-evaluative dimensions — two of four. CHAR-2 Submission shares the relational dimension (both operate in authority relationships) and the volitional dimension. The primary dimension (characterological — comprehensive stable character quality) is the most distinctive of CHAR-5 — most other M09 characteristics are more specifically volitional (CHAR-2), affective (CHAR-3), or both (CHAR-1 humility with volitional primary).
- `T6.7.1` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-6]** E — Confirmed primary dimensions of CHAR-6: *volitional-affective* (primary — the freely-moving will combined with joyful quality of movement); *relational* (Godward — constitutive); *spirit-level* and *heart-level* (constitutional locations). CHAR-2 Submission shares the volitional and relational dimensions — two of four. CHAR-1 Humility shares the relational dimension and partially the volitional. The affective dimension (joyful, gladly-willing) is the most distinctive of CHAR-6 — it connects to M04 (Joy) rather than to other M09 characteristics as its primary dimensional partner. The spirit-level and heart-level locations are shared with CHAR-3 Contrition (also spirit and heart located), confirming these two as deepest-constitutional-level characteristics in M09.
- `T6.7.2` · cluster_finding · /cluster_synthesis — **[CLUSTER]** E — The dimensional sharing pattern reveals a cluster with a clear centre (Humility — CHAR-1 — dimensionally most proximate to the most other members) and a clear periphery (Dignity — CHAR-5 — most distinctively characterological, least dimensionally adjacent to other M09 characteristics). CHAR-1 and CHAR-2 are the most closely related pair — sharing volitional, relational, moral-evaluative, and affective dimensions, confirmed by their Christological co-constitution in Phili2:8. CHAR-1 and CHAR-4 are the second-most proximate pair — sharing volitional, cognitive, and relational dimensions, confirmed by Mat11:29. CHAR-3 and CHAR-6 form an unexpected deep-constitutional pair — both explicitly spirit-and-heart-located (the only two characteristics with explicit spirit-location); both OT-Hebrew-only in their primary vocabulary; both inaccessible to external compulsion at their constitutional level. CHAR-5 is the most dimensionally isolated — its characterological-primary classification is not shared with any other M09 characteristic; it is M09's resting-place rather than movement-characteristic. The M08 structural-opposite relationship (Observation #18) is confirmed by CHAR-5's dimensional isolation — it occupies the same comprehensive character-level as M08's proud self-display but with opposite orientation, making the polarity structurally exact.  ---
- `T6.7.2` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-1]** E — The dimensional sharing pattern confirms the Phase 5/7 analytical structure: CHAR-1 and CHAR-2 are the most closely related characteristics in M09, sharing the volitional, relational, and moral-evaluative dimensions. The shared dimensions explain why Phili2:8 can be the paradigm verse for both (humility in the inner act / submission in the obedient form). The dimensional sharing with CHAR-4 (Meekness) across volitional and relational dimensions explains the Mat11:29 co-occurrence — the same inner posture has both a self-lowering face (humility) and a calibrated-restraint face (meekness). The least dimensional sharing is with CHAR-5 (Dignity) and CHAR-6 (Willing-heartedness) — the latter shares the volitional dimension but with a contrasting direction (willing-heartedness is the will freely moving toward God; humility is the will releasing the claim to standing).
- `T6.7.2` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-2]** E — The dimensional sharing pattern confirms the Phase 5/7 analytical structure: CHAR-1 and CHAR-2 are the most closely related characteristics in M09. The shared volitional, relational, moral-evaluative, and affective dimensions explain their Christological co-constitution (Phili2:3/8; Heb5:8). The carry-forward observation about CHAR-2's M30 register-adjacency (Obedience cluster) is confirmed by the volitional and relational dimensions being the primary ones — M30 will carry the same primary dimensions when built, making the register-adjacency genuinely meaningful rather than merely lexical.
- `T6.7.2` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-3]** E — The dimensional sharing pattern confirms the conceptual proximity of CHAR-1 and CHAR-3 while establishing the affective-existential primary dimension as the key distinguishing feature: CHAR-3 is an affective-existential state (the crushed/broken experience); CHAR-1 is a volitional-dispositional posture. Both occupy the same constitutional locations (spirit, heart) and are both Godward — confirming adjacency. The primary dimension difference (affective-existential vs volitional-dispositional) establishes their analytical distinctness despite their conceptual closeness.
- `T6.7.2` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-4]** E — The dimensional sharing pattern confirms CHAR-4 as most closely related to CHAR-1 — three shared dimensions, the same enabling self-knowledge ground, and the Mat11:29 shared-verse co-designation. The affective dimension (measured compassion — metriopatheō) is CHAR-4's most distinctive feature not shared with CHAR-1's primary dimension (which is volitional-dispositional rather than affective-measured). The dimensional sharing confirms the analytical structure: CHAR-4 is the affective-calibrated relational-engagement expression of the self-aware posture that CHAR-1 names.
- `T6.7.2` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-5]** E — The dimensional sharing pattern confirms CHAR-5 as the most comprehensively characterological characteristic in M09 — its primary dimension (stable comprehensive character quality) is shared less with the other M09 characteristics (which are more specifically volitional, affective, or conditional) than the other characteristics share among themselves. CHAR-5 is the most distinctively character-level characteristic in the cluster; the others are more specifically faculty-engaged (will, affect, spirit). The M08 structural-opposite relationship (carry-forward OPEN) is confirmed by the dimensional analysis: M08's proud self-display is the characterological distortion of what CHAR-5 names — same comprehensive-character level, opposite orientation (assertive vs grounded-settled).
- `T6.7.2` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-6]** E — The dimensional sharing pattern confirms the carry-forward cross-register flags: the affective dimension of CHAR-6 (joyful, gladly-willing) overlaps with M04 (Joy); the volitional dimension overlaps with M29 (Desire/Will); the spirit/heart constitutional location overlaps with CHAR-3 Contrition within M09. The most distinctive feature of CHAR-6 is the *volitional-affective combination* — the will that is also joyful in its movement — which is shared partially with M04 but not fully with any single M09 characteristic. This confirms CHAR-6 as the M09 characteristic most closely related to M04 through its affective-joyful dimension and to M29 through its volitional-freedom dimension.
- `T6.7.3` · cluster_finding · /cluster_synthesis — **[CLUSTER]** E — Dimensional sharing analysis is available from within-session evidence (T6.7.1 and T6.7.2). Programme-wide dimensional data across all clusters would require cross-cluster Phase 9 synthesis data not yet available; findings here draw on the M09 cluster's Phase 9 analytical evidence. The M09-internal dimensional analysis is complete: CHAR-1 as hub, CHAR-5 as periphery, CHAR-1/CHAR-2 and CHAR-1/CHAR-4 as primary proximate pairs, CHAR-3/CHAR-6 as unexpected deep-constitutional pair.  ---  T6 self-evaluation: 1. No re-authoring: did not touch any of the 90 already-present prompts ✓ 5. Distinct findings: no two prompts share an identical opening ✓ → PASS. Proceeding to T7.  ---  ## T7 — gap-fill (14 prompts)
- `T6.7.3` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-1]** E — Dimensional sharing analysis is available from within-session evidence (see T6.7.1 and T6.7.2). Precise dimensional data across all programme characteristics would require cross-cluster Phase 9 synthesis data not yet available; the finding here is based on the evidence accumulated within this characteristic's corpus and the M09 sub-group structure.  ---  T6 self-evaluation:   1.
