**T6.7 · Dimensional Sharing**  (66)

- `T6.7.1` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-1]** E — The primary dimension (will/volition) is shared with CHAR-5 (Refusal to repent — also a volitional characteristic), CHAR-6 (Habitual defection — sustained volitional pattern), and CHAR-4 (Conscience suppression — also operates through the volitional faculty). The conscience dimension (secondary) is shared with CHAR-3 (Confession — conscience names the act) and CHAR-17 (Guilt as inner-being state — guilt as post-act conscience experience). The relational dimension is shared across most M10 characteristics, as the cluster is constituted around relational moral failure.
- `T6.7.1` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-2]** E — The primary dimension (conscience in retrospective mode — T1.8.1) is shared with CHAR-3 (Confession — conscience also drives the confession response), CHAR-17 (Guilt — guilt is conscience's post-act state), and CHAR-4 (Conscience suppression — conscience is the faculty that either engages or is suppressed). The secondary dimension (moral liability / objective guilt — T1.8.3) is shared across all M10 characteristics that involve sinning. The relational dimension (T3.11) is shared broadly across M10's sinning characteristics.
- `T6.7.1` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-3]** E — The primary dimension (conscience in confessional mode) is shared with CHAR-17 (Guilt — conscience in its post-act awareness mode) and CHAR-4 (Conscience suppression — conscience's engagement refused). The secondary dimension (will — which must agree to name the sin) is shared with CHAR-1 (Wilful sinning — will as primary faculty) and CHAR-5 (Refusal to repent — will refusing to turn). The speech dimension is unique to CHAR-3 within M10's characteristics — no other M10 characteristic is constitutively speech-based. The relational dimension (directed toward God or persons) is shared broadly across M10 characteristics.
- `T6.7.1` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-4]** E — The primary dimension (conscience in malfunctioning/absent/refused mode) is shared with CHAR-3 (Confession — conscience in confessional mode) and CHAR-17 (Guilt as inner-being state — conscience in retrospective-awareness mode) — all three are conscience-dimension characteristics, differentiated by the conscience's mode of operation. The secondary cognitive dimension (1Cor 15:34 — knowledge of God absent) is shared with CHAR-2 (Unintentional sinning — knowledge gap). The secondary will dimension (Jer 2:35 — active denial requires will) is shared with CHAR-1 (Wilful sinning) and CHAR-5 (Refusal to repent).
- `T6.7.1` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-5]** E — The primary dimension (will/volition) is shared with CHAR-1 (Wilful sinning — will as the primary faculty) and CHAR-3 (Confession — will as the agreeing faculty). The secondary heart-dimension (2Ki 10:31 — not with all his heart) is shared with CHAR-3's heart-initiation. The constitutional hardening dimension (Neh 9:29 — stiffened neck) has resonance with CHAR-4's constitutional deformation (Tit 3:11 — warped). The generational transmission dimension is shared with CHAR-7 (Contagious sin — leadership transmitting to community).
- `T6.7.1` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-6]** E — The primary dimension (will/volition in dispositional mode) is shared with CHAR-5 (will in decision mode) and CHAR-1 (will in single-act mode) — all three are will-dimension characteristics, differentiated by the temporal mode of the will. The secondary epistemic dimension (1Jo 3:6 — never seen or known him) is shared with CHAR-4 (Conscience suppression — epistemic blindness to sin) though differently configured. The institutional-communal dimension (Hos 8:11) is shared with CHAR-7 (Contagious sin — communal transmission). The generational dimension (Hos 10:9; Pro 13:22) is shared with CHAR-15 (Generational sin).
- `T6.7.1` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-7]** E — The primary dimension (will/volition in causative mode) is shared with CHAR-1 (will in single-act mode) and CHAR-5 (will in sustained non-departure mode) — all three are will-dimension characteristics, differentiated by the temporal mode and the directionality (personal vs causative). The secondary relational dimension (the authority relationship as transmission medium) is unique to CHAR-7 within M10 — no other characteristic defines itself through the authority relationship as the primary medium. The institutional-structural dimension (2Ki 23:15 — physical deposit) is shared with CHAR-6's institutional dimension (Hos 8:11 — altars for sinning).
- `T6.7.1` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-8]** E — The primary dimension (will/volition in its decisive-breaking mode) is shared with CHAR-19 (transgression — the same wilful breaking-away), CHAR-1 (wilful sinning — the same deliberate volitional act), and CHAR-5 (refusal to repent — will in its non-departure mode). The political-social dimension is unique to CHAR-8 within the M10 cluster — no other characteristic defines itself primarily through the political-authority-relationship domain. The "to this day" permanent-fracture dimension is partially shared with CHAR-5's permanent non-departure (both produce lasting conditions), but CHAR-8's fracture is political-structural while CHAR-5's is volitional-personal.
- `T6.7.1` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-9]** E — The heart-dimension (primary) is shared with CHAR-16 (sinner as moral character — constituted by the corrupted heart) and CHAR-1 (wilful sinning — same heart-origin). The will-dimension (secondary) is shared with CHAR-1/5/6. The mouth/body-dimension is unique to CHAR-9 within M10 — no other characteristic defines itself through the mouth as its constitutive body-part expression. The M06 cross-register (contempt-dimension of VCG-01 slander) gives CHAR-9 a dimensional connection to the M06 cluster not shared by other M10 characteristics.
- `T6.7.1` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-10]** E — The desire/appetite dimension (enticement) is shared with M14 (desire/temptation cluster — its primary domain). The identity-performance dimension (hypocrisy) is shared with M08 (pride/hypocrisy cluster). The covenantal-departure dimension (apostasy) is shared with M31 (faith/unbelief cluster). Within M10, the will-dimension is shared with CHAR-1/5/6/8 (all volitional characteristics). The relational-contagion dimension (Gal 2:13's social spread) is shared with CHAR-7.
- `T6.7.1` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-11]** E — The primary dimension (universal structural condition) is unique to CHAR-11 in its full scope — no other M10 characteristic addresses the structural condition of all persons. The sub-dimensions of CHAR-11 are shared across the cluster: the debt/guilt dimension (with CHAR-17 — Guilt as inner-being state), the power/enslavement dimension (with CHAR-12 — Sin as enslaving power), the wickedness-as-character dimension (with CHAR-16 — Sinner as moral character), the forgiveness-remedy dimension (with CHAR-14 — Forgiveness sought and received), the generational-transmission dimension (with CHAR-15 — Generational sin). CHAR-11 shares a dimension with every major sin-related characteristic in the cluster — because the universal condition encompasses every specific dimension.
- `T6.7.1` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-12]** E — The primary dimension (will captive under sin's governing power) is shared with CHAR-11 in its VCG-03 enslaving-power register. The habitual-practice dimension (Joh 8:34; 1Jo 3:8/9) is shared with CHAR-6. The adversarial-spiritual dimension (1Jo 3:8 — the devil; 1Jo 5:18 — the evil one) is the most distinctive dimension to CHAR-12 — it appears most explicitly here of all M10 characteristics studied. The eschatological-death dimension (1Jo 5:16) is shared with CHAR-11's death-consequence register.
- `T6.7.1` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-13]** E — The objective-record dimension (sin retained before God) is unique to CHAR-13 within M10. However: (1) The internal-inscription dimension (Jer 17:1 — heart-engraving) is shared with CHAR-4 (conscience suppression — the heart that has been written upon) and CHAR-17 (guilt — the heart's experiential register); (2) The confession-requiring dimension (Num 5:7; Psa 32:5) is shared with CHAR-3 (confession); (3) The accumulation dimension (1Sa 12:19; Isa 30:1) is shared with CHAR-6 (habitual defection — the pattern generating accumulation); (4) The generational-legacy dimension (VCG-04; Exo 34:7) is shared with any generational-transmission characteristic in M10.
- `T6.7.1` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-14]** E — Three of CHAR-14's dimensions are shared. (1) *The sin-record-addressed dimension* is shared with CHAR-13 (the record that CHAR-14 removes); (2) *The confession-verbal dimension* of seeking is shared with CHAR-3; (3) *The guilt-relief dimension* of receiving is shared with CHAR-17 (guilt as inner-being state — the subjective guilt that forgiveness relieves). CHAR-14's unique dimensions (kip.pu.rim covering mechanism; Christological blood-atonement; the love-magnitude-proportional-to-forgiveness principle) are not shared with other M10 characteristics.
- `T6.7.1` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-15]** E — Three of CHAR-15's dimensions are shared. (1) *The wilful-sinning-acts dimension* (each generation's own sinning) is shared with CHAR-1; (2) *The divine-record dimension* (the accumulated sinning before God) is shared with CHAR-13; (3) *The refusal-to-repent dimension* (the persistent "still sinned") is shared with CHAR-5. CHAR-15's unique dimension — the *lineage-transmission mechanism* (first father's sinning creating the chain; Isa 43:27) — is not shared with any other M10 characteristic studied.
- `T6.7.1` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-16]** E — Three dimensions are shared. (1) *The sin-as-condition dimension* (the universal sinner-standing) is shared with CHAR-11; (2) *The accumulated-acts dimension* (what formed the sinner-character) is shared with CHAR-1; (3) *The forgiveness-seeking dimension* (the conscience-engaged sinner seeking mercy) is shared with CHAR-14. CHAR-16's unique dimension — the *agent-noun identity* (the person constituted as the recognisable moral type "the sinner") — is not shared with any other M10 characteristic studied; it is CHAR-16's distinctive contribution.
- `T6.7.1` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-17]** E — Three dimensions are shared: (1) *The objective-record dimension* (the guilt before God as a registered fact) is shared with CHAR-13; (2) *The confession-producing dimension* (guilt-recognition generating confession) is shared with CHAR-3; (3) *The forgiveness-resolution dimension* (the guilt-state requiring and receiving forgiveness) is shared with CHAR-14. CHAR-17's unique dimension — the *subjective inner experience of guilt* (the felt weight, the shame-posture, the conscience-stagger — pu.qah) — is not shared with any other M10 characteristic. This is the characteristic's irreducible contribution.
- `T6.7.1` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-18]** E — Three dimensions are shared: (1) *The divine-record dimension* (iniquity held in God's memory) is shared with CHAR-13; (2) *The generational-transmission dimension* (VCG-02) is shared with CHAR-15; (3) *The forgiveness-resolution dimension* (VCG-06 — pardon and removal) is shared with CHAR-14. CHAR-18's unique dimensions — (a) the objective-crime accumulation toward divine threshold (Gen 15:16 — "not yet complete"), (b) the heart-seated origin (VCG-08 — Eze 14:3), and (c) the self-destructive-force register (VCG-07 — Pro 5:22) — are not shared with any other M10 characteristic studied. These three constitute CHAR-18's irreducible analytical contribution.
- `T6.7.1` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-19]** E — Three dimensions are shared: (1) *The accumulated-crime dimension* (transgression producing accumulated weight) is shared with CHAR-18; (2) *The confession-producing dimension* (known transgression generating confession) is shared with CHAR-3; (3) *The forgiveness-resolution dimension* (transgression requiring and receiving pardon) is shared with CHAR-14. CHAR-19's unique dimensions — (a) the knowing-boundary-crossing emphasis (Rom 4:15 — requires law), (b) Psa 36:1's pseudo-conscience as inner voice of transgression, and (c) Amos 1:3's threshold-counting formula — are not shared with any other M10 characteristic studied.
- `T6.7.1` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-20]** E — Two dimensions are partially shared: (1) *The covenant-boundary-crossing dimension* (the same act as CHAR-19) — partially shared with CHAR-19 where both analyse the same event (Hos 6:7); (2) *The confession-producing dimension* (faithlessness named and confessed) — partially shared with CHAR-3. CHAR-20's unique dimensions — (a) the spirit-vigilance-requirement (Mal 2:15/16 — guard yourselves in your spirit), (b) the hardening-under-distress dynamic (2Ch 28:22 — yet more faithless), (c) the constitutive-prior-bond requirement (the covenant whose breaking makes faithlessness possible), and (d) the cross-register M13/M31 flagging — are not shared with any other M10 characteristic studied.
- `T6.7.1` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-21]** E — Two dimensions are partially shared: (1) *The accumulated-twisting-as-crime dimension* is partially shared with CHAR-18 (through the three-tier confession formula); (2) *The sinning-act dimension* is partially shared with CHAR-1 where a.vah's twisting produces acts. CHAR-21's unique dimensions — (a) the desire-as-corruption-mechanism (Eph 4:22 — "through deceitful desires"), (b) the three-stage conscience-suppression trajectory (operational → bypassed → eliminated), (c) the cosmic-liberation eschatology (Rom 8:21), and (d) the creative-faculty engagement through evil-devising (Pro 6:14 — "continually devises") — are not shared with any other M10 characteristic studied.
- `T6.7.1` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-22]** E — Two dimensions are partially shared: (1) *The heart-devising dimension* (Psa 58:2) is partially shared with CHAR-21 (perversion of the inner faculty); (2) *The volitional-turn dimension* (Eze 18:24 — turns and commits injustice) is partially shared with CHAR-20 (faithlessness — the covenant-turn). CHAR-22's unique dimensions — (a) the shamelessness diagnostic (Zep 3:5 — "the unjust knows no shame"), (b) the scaling character-consistency (Luk 16:10 — dishonest across all scales), (c) the M26 direct-negation (adikos as "not-just"), and (d) the institutional-social scope (judicial, commercial, civic, cosmic) — are not shared with any other M10 characteristic studied.
- `T6.7.2` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-1]** E — The dimensional sharing reveals that the M10 cluster is structurally unified by a small set of shared dimensions (will, conscience, relational capacity) that appear across multiple characteristics in different configurations. Wilful sinning (CHAR-1) shares its primary volitional dimension with habitual defection (CHAR-6) and refusal to repent (CHAR-5) — all three are volitional characteristics. What distinguishes them is not the faculty (will) but the temporal and developmental configuration: CHAR-1 is the single act; CHAR-6 is the repeated pattern; CHAR-5 is the refusal to reverse. The dimensional sharing reveals M10's characteristics as a developmental spectrum of the same volitional phenomenon.
- `T6.7.2` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-2]** E — The dimensional sharing confirms that CHAR-2's distinctiveness lies not in unique dimensions but in the *temporal configuration* of shared dimensions. Conscience (shared with CHAR-3, CHAR-4, CHAR-17) operates retrospectively in CHAR-2 — after the act, triggered by disclosure. In CHAR-1 it operates concurrently (during the act) and is overridden. In CHAR-4 it operates and is suppressed. The same faculty (conscience), the same dimension, but at different temporal positions in the act-to-response sequence. CHAR-2's analytical identity is precisely this temporal displacement — same dimensions, displaced timing.
- `T6.7.2` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-3]** E — The dimensional sharing confirms that CHAR-3 occupies a *convergence point* in the M10 characteristic landscape: it shares the conscience dimension with CHAR-17 (its input) and CHAR-4 (its failure alternative), the will dimension with CHAR-1 (its precondition) and CHAR-5 (its alternative trajectory), and is uniquely characterised by the speech dimension that none of the others carry. CHAR-3 is thus structurally the characteristic where conscience and will converge in verbal expression — it is the characteristic most fully engaged with all three inner faculties (conscience, will, speech) simultaneously, which explains its central hinge position in the inner-being landscape.
- `T6.7.2` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-4]** E — The dimensional sharing confirms that CHAR-4 is the *conscience-dimension characteristic in its failure mode*: it shares the conscience dimension with CHAR-3 and CHAR-17 but differs in the conscience's operational state — where CHAR-3 has conscience functioning in its speech-act mode and CHAR-17 has conscience in its post-act awareness mode, CHAR-4 has conscience in its absent/denied/numbed mode. The three characteristics together map the full operational spectrum of the conscience faculty: functioning (CHAR-3), experiencing (CHAR-17), and failing (CHAR-4). This confirms CHAR-4's analytical identity as the conscience's failure characteristic within the M10 cluster.
- `T6.7.2` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-5]** E — The dimensional sharing confirms that CHAR-5 is not an isolated characteristic but a *volitional nexus*: it shares the will-dimension with CHAR-1 (sinning) and CHAR-3 (confessing) — the same faculty serves all three, but in different directional configurations. CHAR-5's will *refuses to turn*, CHAR-1's will *commits the wrong*, CHAR-3's will *names the wrong as wrong*. The shared dimension reveals that the will is the decisive inner faculty across the sinning-confession-refusal triad — its direction and mode determine which characteristic is instantiated.
- `T6.7.2` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-6]** E — The dimensional sharing confirms that CHAR-6 occupies a *convergence point* in the M10 landscape: it shares dimensions with CHAR-1 (acts), CHAR-4 (epistemic impairment), CHAR-5 (volitional sustained direction), CHAR-7 (communal transmission), and CHAR-15 (generational consequence). This convergence reflects CHAR-6's position as the *formational endpoint* — the characteristic in which multiple sinning dimensions combine and are consolidated into the settled dispositional pattern. CHAR-6 is where the sinning characteristics converge in their formational consequence.
- `T6.7.2` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-7]** E — The dimensional sharing confirms that CHAR-7 shares the will-dimension with CHAR-1/5/6 but is uniquely defined by the relational-authority dimension that none of the others carry. The authority relationship is CHAR-7's analytically distinctive feature — it is what makes the same personal sinning of CHAR-1 into contagious sin rather than merely personal sin. The dimensional uniqueness of the authority-relationship dimension explains CHAR-7's place in the landscape: it is the characteristic that links the personal sinning characteristics (CHAR-1/5/6) to the communal-scale consequences (CHAR-6 at scale; national abandonment — 1Ki 14:16) through the authority medium.
- `T6.7.2` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-8]** E — The dimensional sharing confirms that CHAR-8 is analytically distinctive primarily through its *domain* (political-authority-relationship) rather than through a unique inner-being faculty. The will-dimension is shared across many M10 sinning characteristics; what makes CHAR-8 distinct is that the will's breaking-away act occurs specifically in the political-governing domain. This domain-specificity is what justifies CHAR-8 as a distinct characteristic: the same inner-being will-faculty operating in the political domain produces distinct relational, social, and theological consequences from its operation in the personal-moral domain (CHAR-19).
- `T6.7.2` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-9]** E — The dimensional sharing confirms CHAR-9's unique position: it shares the heart-origin and will-dimension with several M10 characteristics (CHAR-1/5/6/16) but is uniquely defined by the mouth/speech dimension that none of the other M10 characteristics carries. The mouth-dimension is CHAR-9's analytically distinguishing feature within the cluster — the same inner moral failures that appear across M10 are uniquely constituted as CHAR-9 when they cross through the mouth into speech. The cross-cluster connection to M06 through VCG-01's contempt-dimension shows that sinful speech is at the intersection of M10 (moral failure) and M06 (contempt/hate).
- `T6.7.2` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-10]** E — The dimensional sharing confirms CHAR-10's *gateway character*: each of its three mechanisms has its primary dimensional home in a different cluster (M14, M08, M31), with CHAR-10 holding the M10 dimension of each (the sinful-outcome face of each mechanism). CHAR-10 is the most cross-cluster characteristic in M10 studied so far — three of its four primary dimensions belong primarily to other clusters. Within M10, CHAR-10 captures only the sinful-failure register of mechanisms whose primary conceptual home lies elsewhere.
- `T6.7.2` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-11]** E — The dimensional sharing confirms CHAR-11's unique position: it is the only characteristic that shares dimensions with every other sinning characteristic in M10. This is because CHAR-11 is not a peer-characteristic alongside others but the ground-condition that encompasses them. The other characteristics each focus on one dimension of the universal condition (CHAR-12 — its power-dimension; CHAR-17 — its guilt-dimension; CHAR-15 — its generational-dimension); CHAR-11 encompasses all dimensions simultaneously. The dimensional sharing reveals CHAR-11 as the M10 characteristic's structural foundation rather than one of its members.
- `T6.7.2` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-12]** E — The dimensional sharing reveals that CHAR-12 is the *most adversarially-grounded characteristic in M10*: the adversarial-spiritual dimension (devil's originary sinning, evil one's touch) is most concentrated in this characteristic's corpus. While other dimensions are shared (with CHAR-11, CHAR-6), the devil-ground and evil-one-barring dimensions are unique concentrations in CHAR-12. This positions CHAR-12 as the characteristic that most directly names sin's enslaving power in its spiritual-adversarial source and its liberation in Christ's protective counter-agency.
- `T6.7.2` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-13]** E — The dimensional sharing confirms CHAR-13 as the *archival centre* of the M10 cluster: it shares dimensions with CHAR-1 (the acts that fill it), CHAR-3 (the confession that addresses it), CHAR-4 (the heart-inscription that is its internal form), CHAR-6 (the habits that accumulate it), and CHAR-17 (the guilt that is its inner-experiential face). CHAR-13 is the characteristic that all others feed into or respond to — it is the moral accounting dimension that gives the other characteristics their lasting significance before God.
- `T6.7.2` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-14]** E — The dimensional sharing confirms CHAR-14 as the *resolution hub* of M10: it shares dimensions with CHAR-13 (the record addressed), CHAR-3 (the confession accompanying seeking), and CHAR-17 (the guilt relieved). These shared dimensions name exactly what forgiveness sought and received does in the landscape: it addresses the objective record, encompasses the subjective confession, and relieves the inner guilt. CHAR-14's unique dimensions (kip.pu.rim; blood-covenant; love-magnitude) are the mechanisms and consequences that belong distinctively to the resolution-characteristic.
- `T6.7.2` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-15]** E — The dimensional sharing confirms CHAR-15 as the *temporal-depth layer* of several other M10 characteristics: it adds the generational-historical dimension to CHAR-1's individual sinning acts, CHAR-13's divine record, and CHAR-5's refusal to repent. CHAR-15's unique contribution (the lineage-transmission mechanism) is what distinguishes it from these shared dimensions: no other M10 characteristic addresses how sin moves through family and national belonging across generations as its defining feature.
- `T6.7.2` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-16]** E — The dimensional sharing confirms CHAR-16 as the *characterological face* of M10: its shared dimensions all point to what other M10 characteristics contribute to it (CHAR-1 provides the acts that form it; CHAR-11 provides the condition that grounds it) or what it contributes to them (CHAR-14 receives the seeking that CHAR-16's recognition generates). CHAR-16's unique agent-noun dimension is what converts all these other characteristics into a recognisable person-type: the one who does CHAR-1 acts, stands in CHAR-11's condition, bears CHAR-13's record, is the person CHAR-16 names as "the sinner."
- `T6.7.2` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-17]** E — The dimensional sharing confirms CHAR-17 as the *experiential bridge* of M10: it bridges CHAR-13 (the objective record) to CHAR-3 (the confession) to CHAR-14 (the forgiveness sought). Without CHAR-17's subjective guilt-experience, the conscience would not be activated; without conscience activation, CHAR-3's confession and CHAR-14's seeking would not occur. CHAR-17 is the inner-experiential hinge that connects the objective moral reality (CHAR-13's record) to the remedial response (CHAR-3/14). Its unique subjective dimension (the felt weight, shame, conscience-stagger) is what makes the hinge function.
- `T6.7.2` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-18]** E — The dimensional sharing confirms CHAR-18 as the *substantive moral-crime foundation* of M10: its shared dimensions all point to what other characteristics do with the iniquity it names (CHAR-13 holds it, CHAR-15 transmits it, CHAR-14 forgives it). CHAR-18's unique dimensions (accumulation-to-threshold, heart-seated origin, self-destructive force) are what make the iniquity itself distinct from mere acts (CHAR-1) or felt guilt (CHAR-17): it is a measured, heart-originated, self-destructive crime-accumulation — a category of moral reality that requires its own characteristic to describe.
- `T6.7.2` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-19]** E — The dimensional sharing confirms CHAR-19 as the *volitional-act gateway* of M10: its shared dimensions all point to what other characteristics do with what CHAR-19 produces (CHAR-18 accumulates it, CHAR-3 confesses it, CHAR-14 forgives it). CHAR-19's unique dimensions — the knowing-requirement (Rom 4:15), the inner-voice displacement of conscience (Psa 36:1), and the counted-threshold structure (Amos 1:3) — are what make transgression irreducible within M10: these cannot be derived from any other characteristic.
- `T6.7.2` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-20]** E — The minimal dimensional sharing confirms CHAR-20 as the most analytically distinct characteristic in M10: its unique dimensions (spirit-vigilance command, hardening-under-distress, prior-bond requirement, M13/M31 cross-register) are not derivable from any other M10 characteristic. The characteristic adds what no other M10 characteristic supplies: the constitutive covenantal-relational frame that gives M10's moral failures their deepest interpersonal weight. Every other M10 characteristic can be described in terms of acts, conditions, records, or identities; CHAR-20 requires a covenant-bond to have been and now be broken.
- `T6.7.2` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-21]** E — The minimal dimensional sharing confirms CHAR-21 as the most constitutionally fundamental characteristic in M10: while other M10 characteristics describe acts, records, identities, and feelings, CHAR-21 describes the distorted inner instrument from which all the others proceed. The unique dimensions (desire-mechanism, conscience-elimination, cosmic scope, creative evil-devising) are what make CHAR-21 irreducible — they describe the inner moral faculty in its structural distortion, not any specific expression of that distortion.
- `T6.7.2` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-22]** E — The minimal dimensional sharing confirms CHAR-22 as analytically distinct within M10: its unique dimensions (shamelessness as diagnostic, scaling character-consistency, M26-interface, institutional scope) describe injustice's specific other-directed character that no other M10 characteristic covers. CHAR-22 is M10's conduct-toward-others characteristic — it is the only M10 characteristic that is primarily defined by what is done to other persons and to social institutions rather than primarily by what happens within the inner person.

### M10b  (112 findings)

**T1.2.3 · Best working description**  (7)

- `T1.2.3` · cluster_finding · /cluster_synthesis — **[CLUSTER]** E — At cluster scope, the best working description of M10b is: a cluster of six analytically distinct inner-being evil-forms that together describe the complete inner-being profile of the human person in rebellion against the moral order — covering the person's identity (Char 1), constitutional-generative nature (Char 2), the divine-verdict on their moral character and idolatrous devotion (Chars 3-4), the active cognitive-volitional process through which evil is produced from within (Char 5), and the speech-expressive mode through which inner wickedness becomes socially operative (Char 6). The six characteristics are related by their shared subject-matter (inner evil in its multiple forms) and their shared structural opposition to righteousness and the divine character, but are analytically distinguishable by their primary dimensions: Char 1 (volitional-dispositional), Char 2 (constitutional-structural), Char 3 (relational-moral verdict), Char 4 (volitional-devotional), Char 5 (cognitive-volitional-generative), Char 6 (volitional-expressive).
- `T1.2.3` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-1]** E — Based on the 190-verse corpus, the working description is: Wickedness-as-settled-person-identity is a stable inner-being disposition in which the human person's constitutive faculties — will, soul, heart, moral perception — are organised around an orientation away from God and toward evil, injustice, and harm. The disposition is habitual and identity-defining: it generates the person's characteristic conduct, determines their relationship to God (non-service, Mal 3:18; forgetting, Psa 9:17; relational estrangement, Pro 15:29), shapes their relationships with others (exploitation, concealment, predation — VCGs 03, 08, 09), and carries within itself an inherent instability that moves toward dissolution and judgment. It is a constitutional orientation of the whole person, not a momentary state, distinguishable from the righteous person at the deepest inner level (Psa 7:9: "you who test the minds and hearts").
- `T1.2.3` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-2]** E — Evil as constitutional inner nature is the structural condition of the human inner person in which the generative architecture of the heart, mind, and soul is oriented toward the production of evil as its default output. The heart is a treasury from which evil is actively drawn (Mat 12:35 — VCG-03; Luk 6:45 — VCG-03), all evil things come from within (Mar 7:23 — VCG-03), and the inner nature is diseased in a way that makes good fruit constitutionally impossible without transformation (Mat 7:17-18 — VCG-02). The condition saturates the person at multiple levels — the soul is filled with vice clusters (Rom 1:29 — VCG-05), the conscience is defiled (Heb 10:22 — VCG-06), and the mind is hostile (Col 1:21 — VCG-06). The characteristic is NT-distinctive: the Hebrew OT (Characteristic 1) identified the wicked person by their inner orientation; the NT (Characteristic 2) identifies the constitutional evil as the baseline condition of human nature from which that orientation flows.
- `T1.2.3` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-3]** E — Abomination as divine revulsion on moral character is the verdict and response of divine holiness to specific inner moral conditions and their expressions — a verdict of active revulsion that names certain qualities of the heart, will, conscience, and conduct as fundamentally incompatible with the divine moral order. The evidence shows the characteristic operating at two poles: the divine-inner response (God's revulsion — Pro 11:1; Pro 16:5; Luk 16:15) and the human-inner state (the abominable condition — crooked heart, arrogant heart, silenced conscience, dishonest dealing). Between these poles lies the three-part mechanism: the act or inner condition that defiles, the sacred boundary it transgresses, and the divine revulsion that responds. The 40-verse corpus documents the characteristic across commercial practice (VCG-01), inner character (VCG-02), worship (VCG-03), conscience (VCG-04), and identity (VCG-05), showing it as a comprehensive category of divine moral response to human inner-being failure.
- `T1.2.3` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-4]** E — Idolatrous abomination is the dispositional condition of the inner person in which the devotion-capacity — heart, soul, eyes, will — is attached to and oriented toward idol-objects rather than God, producing an accumulated inner-and-outer pattern of abominable practices that provokes divine revulsion, drives the divine presence away, and progressively transforms the person's character toward the likeness of what they love. The evidence documents this across: the heart going after idols (Eze 11:21 — VCG-05), the soul delighting in abominations (Isa 66:3 — VCG-04), the eyes feasting on detestable things (Eze 20:7 — VCG-05), and the will persistently refusing to turn from them (Eze 20:8 — VCG-05: "none of them cast away the detestable things their eyes feasted on"). The divine response is withdrawal (Eze 5:11 — VCG-05) and eventually unprecedented judgment (Eze 5:9 — VCG-05: "what I have never yet done").
- `T1.2.3` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-5]** E — Iniquity as active inner scheming and evil generation is an ongoing inner-moral process in which the mind, heart, and will generate evil from within — devising harmful schemes, harbouring accumulated iniquity, and expressing a settled character-type whose habitual pattern is the working of evil against others and against God. The 79-verse corpus documents this across: active inner generation (devising, conceiving, plotting — VCG-02, 14V); habitual character-definition of the evildoer class (VCG-03, 24V); the stored-and-harboured inner condition (VCG-04, 23V); the evil quality of the deeds that express the inner condition (VCG-05, 11V); and the trouble the characteristic inflicts on victims (VCG-01, 7V). The primary inner-being location is the heart and thought-life (Isa 32:6 — VCG-02: "heart busy with iniquity"; Isa 59:7 — VCG-02: "thoughts are thoughts of iniquity"); the primary impact is harmful scheming directed against others and the severing of the divine-human relationship (Psa 66:18 — VCG-04: cherished iniquity forecloses divine hearing).
- `T1.2.3` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-6]** E — Evil expressed through speech is the socially operative expression of inner wickedness through the instrument of speech — spanning three modes: (1) direct defiant contempt for God uttered verbally by the hardened will under judgment (VCG-01); (2) harmful speech that dishonours God's name or damages persons' reputations through misrepresentation (VCG-02); (3) hostile false accusation that charges the innocent as evildoers through legal-social processes (VCG-03). The characteristic's inner content varies by VCG — from total will-defiance against God (Rev 16:9 — VCG-01) to malicious hostile judgment (Joh 18:30 — VCG-03) to reckless denunciation (1Cor 10:30 — VCG-02) — but the common thread is inner wickedness finding its socially operative form through speech: blasphemy, slander, false accusation, or harmful denunciation.

**T1.8 · Dimension Classification**  (21)

- `T1.8.1` · cluster_finding · /cluster_synthesis — **[CLUSTER]** E — The six characteristics' primary dimensions collectively reveal the cluster's dimensional architecture. Char 1: volitional-dispositional (the will's settled orientation). Char 2: constitutional-structural (the inner nature's generative constitution). Char 3: relational-moral (divine revulsion as the constitutive response). Char 4: volitional-devotional (the will's directional devotional choice). Char 5: cognitive-volitional-generative (the deliberate scheming of heart and mind). Char 6: volitional-expressive (the will's wickedness enacted through speech). The cluster reveals that the will (volition) is the most frequently occurring primary-dimension element — appearing in Chars 1, 4, 5, and 6 as the central locus of inner evil. Char 2 (constitutional-structural) is the exception, placing the primary locus in the constitutive nature rather than the volitional faculty. Char 3 (relational-moral) is the unique case where the primary dimension is defined by the divine responding party rather than the human inner state. The cluster's dominant primary dimension is volitional — M10b is predominantly about the will's wrong orientation, misdirected devotion, scheming enactment, and speech-expression of inner wickedness.
- `T1.8.1` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-1]** E — The primary dimension is volitional-dispositional: wickedness-as-settled-person-identity is fundamentally a settled orientation of the will — a direction the will has taken and consolidated. The evidence is concentrated here: Eze 3:18; 33:8; 33:11 use "way" (derek) as the primary metaphor, consistently indicating a volitional direction. Isa 55:7 calls for the wicked to "forsake his way and the unrighteous man his thoughts" — the volitional dimension is the primary address for potential reversal. Pro 4:14 ("do not enter the path of the wicked") frames the characteristic as a volitional entry. The God-exclusion (Psa 10:4; 10:13) is volitional — the wicked "renounce God" from within their heart. This is a dimension of active will, not merely a passive condition imposed from outside.
- `T1.8.1` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-2]** E — The primary dimension is constitutional-structural: evil as constitutional inner nature is first and foremost a structural condition of the inner person — the state the inner architecture is in by default. The evidence from VCG-02 and VCG-03 is definitional: the tree-nature metaphor (Mat 7:17-18) names a constitutional-structural condition that determines output; the heart-as-treasury (Mat 12:35; Luk 6:45) names a structural reservoir from which evil is actively drawn. This differs from the volitional-dispositional primary dimension of CHAR-1: CHAR-2's primary reference is not "the direction the will has taken" but "the structural condition from which all output flows." The constitutional-condition classification is the most analytically accurate primary dimension.
- `T1.8.1` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-3]** E — The primary dimension is relational-moral: the characteristic is constitutively defined by the divine-human relational verdict of revulsion in response to inner moral conditions. Every to.e.vah statement is framed relationally — "an abomination to the Lord," "an abomination to the righteous" — the characteristic cannot be defined without reference to the responding other. This is distinct from the volitional-dispositional primary of CHAR-1 or the constitutional-structural primary of CHAR-2. CHAR-3's primary dimension is the relational-evaluative encounter between divine holiness and human inner moral character, in which divine revulsion is the constitutive response. Supporting evidence: Pro 3:32 (vc=1686 — VCG-02): the devious person is an abomination and excluded from divine confidence — the relational consequence is definitional; Luk 16:15 (vc=21 — VCG-02): what is exalted among men is an abomination in the sight of God — the divine sight/relational perspective is what defines the category.
- `T1.8.1` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-4]** E — The primary dimension is volitional-devotional: idolatrous abomination is fundamentally the will's directional choice and sustained commitment to idol-objects as the object of the devotion-capacity. The Ezekiel VCG-05 corpus (43V) consistently frames the characteristic as a directional volitional commitment: Eze 11:21 (vc=1631 — VCG-05): "heart goes after" — directional volitional movement; Eze 20:8 (vc=65911 — VCG-05): "not willing to listen… none of them cast away" — volitional refusal to depart; Eze 14:6 context parallel: "turn away your faces from all your abominations" — the volitional turn is the prescribed address. The Deuteronomic VCG-02 corpus (14V) addresses the will directly: Deu 18:9 (VCG-02 — "shall not learn to follow"), Deu 7:25 (VCG-02 — "shall not covet"). The characteristic's primary dimension is the will's devotional direction.
- `T1.8.1` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-5]** E — The primary dimension is cognitive-volitional-generative: iniquity as active inner scheming operates primarily through the combined engagement of the cognitive faculty (devising, planning, thinking) and the volitional faculty (choosing, refusing to reject evil, executing at dawn). VCG-02 (14V — the deliberate inner generation register) is the most definitionally central sub-corpus. Isa 59:7 (vc=38203 — VCG-02): "their thoughts are thoughts of iniquity" — the cognitive dimension. Mic 2:1 (vc=38217 — VCG-02): "devise wickedness… when the morning dawns, they perform it" — the volitional execution following the cognitive devising. Isa 32:6 (vc=38181 — VCG-02): "his heart is busy with iniquity" — the heart's generative activity as the productive centre. The primary dimension is thus: volitional-generative (the will's deliberate commitment to producing evil from within through cognitive scheming).
- `T1.8.1` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-6]** E — The primary dimension is volitional-expressive: the characteristic is constitutively the will's expression of wickedness through the speech-instrument. The will is the primary faculty — VCG-01 is the definitional core: Rev 16:9 (vc=14352 — VCG-01): "they did not repent" — the will's refusal is the central inner-being reality; Rev 16:11 (vc=14350 — VCG-01): "they did not repent of their deeds" — the will is the faculty whose orientation produces or refuses to produce the characteristic-reversing act of repentance. Jude 8 (vc=14354 — VCG-01): "reject authority" — the will's authority-rejection is the inner source of the blasphemous speech. The expression through speech is the characteristic's defining mode — speech is not incidental but essential. The primary dimension is therefore volitional-expressive: will-wickedness enacted through the speech faculty.
- `T1.8.2` · cluster_finding · /cluster_synthesis — **[CLUSTER]** E — Supporting the volitional dominance across the cluster: Char 1 (Isa 55:7 — "let the wicked forsake his way" — addressed to the will; Eze 18:21 — "if a wicked person turns away from all the sins" — volitional reversal); Char 4 (Eze 20:8 — "not willing to listen; none of them cast away" — explicit volitional refusal; Isa 66:3 — "they have chosen their own ways" — volitional choice as the mechanism); Char 5 (Job 36:21 — "this you have chosen rather than affliction" — volitional choice of iniquity); Char 6 (Rev 16:9/11 — "they did not repent" — volitional refusal as the defining inner act). Char 2's constitutional-structural primary is supported by Mat 7:11 ("you who are evil" — the state from which good gifts are given anyway, implying structural condition rather than volitional moment) and Mat 12:35 (the evil treasury as the inner constitutional source). The cluster's evidence confirms volition as the dominant primary dimension while acknowledging Char 2's constitutional distinctiveness.
- `T1.8.2` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-1]** E — Supporting the volitional-dispositional classification: (1) the "way" metaphor throughout VCG-07 (22V) — Eze 3:18; 33:8, 11, 12, 14, 15, 19; Pro 4:14, 4:19; Isa 55:7 — consistently locating the characteristic as a direction one travels; (2) the Ezekiel repentance corpus (Eze 18:21–27; 33:12–19) shows that reversal occurs at exactly the volitional level — "turns away from all his sins and does what is just and right"; (3) Pro 21:10 ("the soul of the wicked desires evil") — even the soul-level desire is oriented, not merely inert; (4) the practical-atheism cluster (VCG-02, 8V) uses volitional language — "does not seek" (Psa 10:4), "renounces God" (Psa 10:13), "forgetting God" (Psa 9:17). The act of exclusion is consistently volitional.
- `T1.8.2` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-2]** E — Supporting the constitutional-structural classification: (1) Mat 7:17-18 (vc=14439-14440 — VCG-02): the tree-nature language — "a diseased tree cannot bear good fruit" — is constitutional, not volitional; (2) Mat 7:11 and Luk 11:13 (vc=14438, 14445 — VCG-02): "you who are evil" — the use of "are" addresses a constitutive state, not a chosen direction; (3) Mar 7:23 (vc=14422 — VCG-03): "all these evil things come from within" — the inside-out direction of causation is constitutional; (4) Rom 1:29 (vc=14337 etc. — VCG-05): "filled with all manner of unrighteousness" — saturation is a structural condition, not a volitional act; (5) Heb 10:22 (vc=14426 — VCG-06): "evil conscience" as a structural inner-faculty state that blocks approach to God. The constitutional evidence is the dominant framing of the characteristic's 90-verse corpus.
- `T1.8.2` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-3]** E — Supporting the relational-moral primary: (1) every instance of the characteristic in the corpus is framed as "abomination to the Lord / to the righteous" — never as "this is abominable in itself" without reference to a responding subject; (2) Pro 3:32 (vc=1686 — VCG-02): the relational consequence (exclusion from divine confidence) is presented as co-definitional with the abomination category; (3) Pro 29:27 (vc=65959 — VCG-01): the mutual abomination of wicked and righteous shows the characteristic as constitutively relational — each is an abomination to the other, showing that abomination names a relationship of fundamental moral incompatibility; (4) Luk 16:15 (vc=21 — VCG-02): the explicit reference to God's sight as the defining perspective confirms the relational-evaluative framing; (5) Eze 9:4 (vc=1698 — VCG-04): those who grieve over abominations mirror the divine relational response — the characteristic propagates through the relational chain of moral revulsion.
- `T1.8.2` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-4]** E — Supporting the volitional-devotional primary: (1) the Deuteronomic volitional language (VCG-02) — "shall not covet" (Deu 7:25), "shall not learn to follow" (Deu 18:9), "shall not worship" (Deu 12:31) — all addressing the will directly; (2) Eze 11:21 (vc=1631 — VCG-05): "heart goes after their detestable things" — the heart's directional faculty as the primary location; (3) Eze 20:8 (vc=65911 — VCG-05): "not willing to listen; none of them cast away" — volitional refusal as the characteristic's defining inner stance; (4) Isa 66:3 (vc=1633 — VCG-04): "they have chosen their own ways" — volitional choice is the stated mechanism; (5) the reform narratives (VCG-03): kings' wills are what install or purge the abominations — the characteristically volitional-institutional dimension.
- `T1.8.2` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-5]** E — Supporting the cognitive-volitional-generative primary: (1) the bed-plotting evidence (Psa 36:4 — VCG-02: "plots trouble while on his bed"; Mic 2:1 — VCG-02: "devise wickedness… on their beds") shows deliberate premeditated inner planning; (2) the conceive-and-birth metaphor (Psa 7:14 — VCG-02; Job 15:35 — VCG-02; Isa 59:4 — VCG-02) frames iniquity as the generative output of deliberate inner process; (3) the "heart busy with" evidence (Isa 32:6 — VCG-02) names the heart's productive activity as the locus; (4) the volitional choice evidence (Job 36:21 — VCG-04: "for this you have chosen rather than affliction"; Isa 29:20 — VCG-02: "all who watch to do evil") shows deliberate volitional commitment; (5) the warning-to-the-will evidence (Job 36:10 — VCG-04: return from iniquity; Psa 119:133 — VCG-04: resist dominion) confirms the will as the primary faculty the characteristic inhabits.
- `T1.8.2` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-6]** E — Supporting the volitional-expressive primary: (1) VCG-01's repeated "did not repent" (Rev 16:9; 16:11 — VCG-01) shows the volitional refusal as the characteristic's constitutive inner reality; (2) Jude 8 (vc=14354 — VCG-01): "reject authority" — explicit volitional rejection as the source of blasphemous expression; (3) Rev 13:6 (vc=14356 — VCG-01): the beast "opened its mouth" — the deliberate opening of the speech-instrument is a volitional act; (4) Joh 18:30 (vc=14378 — VCG-03): the accusers' deliberate verbal charge — a volitional speech act expressing malicious inner judgment; (5) Rom 3:8 (vc=14358 — VCG-02): the false attribution is "slanderously charged" — a deliberate verbal act. All primary evidence confirms the characteristic as volitionally enacted through speech.
- `T1.8.3` · cluster_finding · /cluster_synthesis — **[CLUSTER]** E — All six characteristics carry secondary dimensions, and cross-characteristic secondary-dimension sharing reveals the cluster's analytical depth. Dispositional secondary (shared by Chars 1, 3, and 6): the settled inner character-disposition that persists beyond individual acts. Cognitive secondary (shared by Chars 1, 5, and 6): the thought-life and cognitive faculties engaged in evil production (Char 5: Isa 59:7 — "thoughts are thoughts of iniquity"; Char 6: Jude 10 — blaspheming what is not understood). Affective secondary (shared by Chars 4, 5, and 6): the soul/affect attached to abomination, devouring iniquity, or sustaining contemptuous defiance. Constitutional-transformative secondary (shared by Chars 4 and 5): the characteristic reshapes the person's inner character toward itself over time (Char 4: Hos 9:10 — "became detestable like the thing they loved"; Char 5: Pro 30:20 — conscience deadened by sustained iniquity). The cluster reveals that secondary dimensions are the overlapping zones between characteristics — where Char 4's affective inversion meets Char 5's conscience-deadening, and where Char 6's volitional-expressive meets Char 1's dispositional, the cluster's internal connectivity is most visible.  ---  EOF echo "T1 written" ## T2 — Constitutional Location
- `T1.8.3` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-1]** E — Secondary dimensions present in the evidence: (1) affective — the wicked person's soul actively desires evil (Pro 21:10; VCG-09), their inner hostility is visceral (Psa 37:12 — "gnashes his teeth"), their joy is momentary and unstable (Job 20:5; VCG-10) — the affective faculty is engaged and corrupted; (2) cognitive — the deliberative faculty is characterised by deceit (Pro 12:5; VCG-08), practical atheism (VCG-02), moral blindness (Pro 4:19), and morally alien counsel (Job 21:16; VCG-10) — cognition is a secondary dimension; (3) self-perpetuating momentum — a quasi-mechanical dimension in which the characteristic generates its own fuel (Pro 5:22; Pro 10:16 "the gain of the wicked leads to sin" — self-perpetuating momentum). This dispositional secondary dimension is supported but does not displace the primary volitional-generative classification; (2) accumulated-condition — VCG-04's stored/harboured iniquity (Psa 66:18 — VCG-04: cherished in heart; Psa 41:6 — VCG-04: heart gathers iniquity) shows a secondary accumulated-condition dimension; (3) moral-quality-of-deeds — VCG-05's ro.a sub-corpus names the evil quality of expressed deeds as a secondary dimension. **T2.8 · Body-Deposit (DNA / generational)**  (21)

- `T2.8.1` · cluster_finding · /cluster_synthesis — **[CLUSTER]** E — All six characteristics document a constitutional deposit from sustained operation. The cluster's deposits are: Char 1 (the settled inner orientation — the "wicked way" as an established trajectory: Job 22:15); Char 2 (the constitutionalised evil nature deepening as a structural condition over time); Char 3 (conscience-silencing as sustained deposit — Jer 6:15/8:12 — "did not know how to blush"); Char 4 (constitutional character-transformation toward the idol — Hos 9:10: "became detestable like the thing they loved" — the most extreme constitutional deposit in the cluster); Char 5 (worn inner path — Job 22:15; conscience deadened — Pro 30:20; iniquity's dominion — Psa 119:133); Char 6 (will-hardening as deposit — Rev 16:9/11/21 — each judgment deepens the defiance rather than breaking it). The cluster's most extreme constitutional deposit is Char 4 (actual constitutional transformation toward the idol's image) — the characteristic that produces the deepest inner reshaping. Char 6's will-hardening deposit is the most visible under maximum pressure (the Rev 16 sequence).
- `T2.8.1` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-1]** E — The evidence surfaces two forms of sustained-operation deposit. (1) Social-structural deposit: wickedness becomes institutionally embedded — Hab 1:4 (VCG-03): "the wicked surround the righteous; so justice goes forth perverted" — the wicked's sustained operation reshapes the structural environment so that justice is systematically distorted. (2) Self-reinforcing bondage: Pro 5:22 (VCG-06) — "the iniquities of the wicked ensnare him, and he is held fast in the cords of his sin" — the sustained operation of wickedness produces a material-metaphorical constitutional deposit in the form of self-generated bondage. (3) Generational transmission: Num 16:26 (VCG-04) — "touch nothing of theirs, lest you be swept away with all their sins" — the sustained operation of wickedness in a community (Korah's men) creates a contagious-dangerous deposit that threatens proximity; the verse implies generational/communal consequence. The evidence is not explicit about DNA-level or generational biological deposit.
- `T2.8.1` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-2]** E — The evidence surfaces two forms of sustained-operation deposit. (1) Self-reinforcing progressive deterioration: 2Ti 3:13 (vc=14451 — VCG-06): "evil people and impostors will go on from bad to worse" — the characteristic's sustained operation produces a deepening deposit of moral corruption in the person's trajectory. (2) Expanding inner occupation: Mat 12:45 (vc=65879 — VCG-06): the vacated inner space refilled with "seven other spirits more evil than itself" — sustained and then worsened inner occupation; the last state is worse than the first. (3) The spreading-leaven dynamic: 1Cor 5:8 (vc=14363/14412 — VCG-05): kakia and ponēria as old leaven — the leaven metaphor implies that the deposit spreads and corrupts over time, progressively affecting more of the whole. The evidence does not specifically address DNA-level or generational biological deposit.
- `T2.8.1` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-3]** E — The evidence documents a conscience-level deposit. Jer 6:15 (vc=1680 — VCG-04) and Jer 8:12 (vc=1696 — VCG-04) show a state where sustained commission of abomination has produced a permanently silenced conscience — the faculty that should produce shame "did not know how to blush." The sustained deposit is the functional elimination of the conscience's shame-producing capacity. Eze 44:13 (vc=66003 — VCG-04) shows a different deposit: the abominations "that they have committed" become a permanent weight of shame they "shall bear" — the abominations are retained as a personal burden. Ezr 9:11 (vc=66005 — VCG-04): abominations filling the land "from end to end" shows a communal-environmental deposit through which the moral contamination becomes embedded in shared space. The primary individual deposit is the silenced conscience; the secondary communal deposit is the saturating moral contamination of shared space.
- `T2.8.1` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-4]** E — Yes. The evidence documents two forms of sustained deposit. (1) Constitutional character-transformation: Hos 9:10 (vc=1632 — VCG-04): "they became detestable like the thing they loved" — sustained idolatrous devotion deposits a transformed character in the person, making them in inner being what the idol is. This is a constitutional deposit in the deepest sense: the inner character is re-shaped by what it worships. (2) Land defilement as collective-environmental deposit: Eze 33:29 (vc=65999 — VCG-05): "the land a desolation and a waste because of all their abominations"; Jer 16:18 (vc=65964 — VCG-04): "have filled my inheritance with their abominations" — the sustained collective idolatrous abomination deposits a moral-spiritual defilement in the shared environment, creating a legacy that shapes subsequent generations.
- `T2.8.1` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-5]** E — The evidence documents a conscience-deposit and a moral-trajectory deposit. (1) Conscience deadening: Pro 30:20 (vc=38222 — VCG-04): the adulteress who wipes her mouth and says "I have done no wrong" — sustained commission of iniquity without arrest has entirely silenced the conscience; the inner moral faculty no longer registers the wrongdoing. This is a conscience-capacity deposit: the repeated commission deposits a progressive insensitivity. (2) The worn inner path: Job 22:15 (vc=38210 — VCG-04): "the old way that wicked men have trod" — sustained practice of iniquity deposits a worn inner trajectory — a habitual moral path that becomes the default direction. (3) Iniquity's dominion as sustained deposit: Psa 119:133 (vc=38227 — VCG-04): iniquity seeking "dominion" — the sustained inner presence of iniquity accumulates toward gaining controlling influence over the will.
- `T2.8.1` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-6]** E — The evidence documents a will-hardening deposit. Rev 16:9 → 16:11 → 16:21 (VCG-01): across three separate escalating divine judgments, the inner posture intensifies rather than yields — each judgment compounds the defiance rather than breaking it. The sustained operation of the characteristic deposits a progressive hardening of the will against both God and the possibility of repentance. Rev 16:11 (vc=14350 — VCG-01): "they did not repent of their deeds" — not repenting of deeds is the inner evidence of the deposit: the sustained characteristic has produced a will that can no longer orient itself toward repentance even under maximum pressure.
- `T2.8.2` · cluster_finding · /cluster_synthesis — **[CLUSTER]** E — Supporting deposits across the cluster: all six characteristics have abundant evidence for progressive inner deterioration and deposit (see T2.8.1 citations). Contradicting or limiting the deposit's permanence: all six characteristics also evidence reversibility through divine action — Char 1 (Eze 18:21 — if the wicked person turns away from all sins), Char 2 (Act 3:26 — turning from wickedness through divine blessing), Char 3 (Eze 36:31 — divine memory-restoration enabling self-loathing and return), Char 4 (Eze 37:23 — divine cleansing; Jer 4:1 — if you remove detestable things), Char 5 (Isa 55:7 — forsake his thoughts and return), Char 6 (repentance possible but not taken — Rev 16:9). The cluster's unanimous pattern: deposits are real and progressive, but not permanently fixed before the eschatological terminus; divine action and human volitional response can reverse the trajectory at any point prior to the terminal condition.
- `T2.8.2` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-1]** E — Supporting deposit: Pro 5:22 (self-generated bondage), Num 16:26 (contagious community-level consequence), Hab 1:4 (structural perversion of justice), Eze 7:21 (VCG-03 — the wicked as instruments of profanation in God's judgment — the deposit is that they become instruments of his judgment, affecting others beyond themselves). Contra-deposit (or limiting the deposit): Eze 18:20 (VCG-07) explicitly limits generational deposit — "the son shall not suffer for the iniquity of the father, nor the father suffer for the iniquity of the son; the wickedness of the wicked shall be upon himself" — this is a theologically significant limit on the deposit concept. The evidence thus presents both a social-structural deposit and an individual-moral-accountability constraint on that deposit.
- `T2.8.2` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-2]** E — Supporting deposit: 2Ti 3:13 (progressive deterioration), Mat 12:45 (deepening inner occupation), 1Cor 5:8 (spreading leaven). The spreading-leaven metaphor is particularly significant: leaven in its original context means the corruption spreads through the whole batch — the deposit expands. The community-level spread is evidenced in 1Cor 5:6 (cited by context of 1Cor 5:8 — "a little leaven leavens the whole lump"). The individual deposit is the progressively deteriorating constitutional state. Contra-deposit or limiting evidence: the evidence for reversal (Act 3:26 — VCG-05: turning from wickedness; Heb 10:22 — VCG-06: hearts sprinkled clean from an evil conscience; Tit 3:3-4: former malice replaced through grace) shows the deposit is not fixed or deterministic — it can be addressed by divine intervention. The deposit is real but not permanently binding.
- `T2.8.2` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-3]** E — Supporting deposit: Jer 6:15; 8:12 (VCG-04 — conscience wholly silenced as sustained deposit), Eze 44:13 (VCG-04 — abominations as permanent weight), Ezr 9:11 (VCG-04 — communal saturation). Contradicting (or limiting) the permanence of the deposit: Eze 36:31 (vc=1681 — VCG-04): "you will remember your evil ways… and will loathe yourselves for your iniquities and your abominations" — the memory of abominations and the self-loathing conscience can be restored through divine action; Eze 14:6 (vc=65984 — VCG-04): "repent and turn away from your idols" — the call to repentance shows the conscience-deposit is not permanently fixed. Act 3:26 context (from CHAR-2's corpus) is not in this evidence base — within CHAR-3's 40 verses, the limiting evidence is the Ezekiel repentance/restoration passages. The deposit is real and serious but not deterministically fixed.
- `T2.8.2` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-4]** E — Supporting deposit: Hos 9:10 (VCG-04 — constitutional character transformation), Jer 16:18 (VCG-04 — land filled with abominations), Eze 33:29 (VCG-05 — desolation as consequence of accumulated abominations), Eze 20:4 (vc=65994 — VCG-05 — "abominations of their fathers" as a legacy requiring confrontation). Contra-deposit (limiting): Eze 37:23 (vc=65913 — VCG-05): divine cleansing removes the deposit — "I will save them from all the backslidings in which they have sinned, and will cleanse them" — the deposit is real but not permanently fixed; divine intervention can remove it. The deposit is serious and generationally significant but not absolutely irreversible.
- `T2.8.2` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-5]** E — Supporting deposit: Pro 30:20 (VCG-04 — conscience silenced as deposit), Job 22:15 (VCG-04 — worn path as deposit), Psa 119:133 (VCG-04 — dominion-seeking as deposit), Psa 36:12 (vc=38233 — VCG-04 — irrecoverable collapse as terminus of the deposit trajectory). Contra-deposit: Job 36:10 (vc=38215 — VCG-04): "He opens their ears to instruction and commands that they return from iniquity" — divine action can reverse the trajectory; Job 11:14 (vc=38207 — VCG-04): "put it far away" — the call to remove iniquity implies the deposit is not permanently fixed; Jer 4:14 (vc=38205 — VCG-02): "wash your heart from evil" — the washing-command implies reversibility. The deposit is real and progressive but remains reversible through divine action and volitional response.
- `T2.8.2` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-6]** E — Supporting deposit: Rev 16:9/11/21 (VCG-01 — escalating hardening across three plagues); Jude 8 (vc=14354 — VCG-01: dreamers who have already settled into their authority-rejecting posture). Contradicting or limiting: 1Pe 2:12 (vc=14377 — VCG-03): the false accusers who will "see your good deeds and glorify God on the day of visitation" — this implies the possibility of reversal for at least some false accusers; the deposit for VCG-03 false accusation is less absolute than for VCG-01 hardened blasphemy. The deposit is most clearly evidenced for the hardened-will VCG-01 blasphemy; VCG-02/03 false accusation may be more reversible.
- `T2.8.3` · cluster_finding · /cluster_synthesis — **[CLUSTER]** E — Constitutional deposits are evidenced across all six characteristics. This prompt is satisfied.
- `T2.8.3` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-1]** E — The evidence is not silent; deposits are evidenced with both supporting and limiting data (T2.8.1–T2.8.2). The primary constitutional deposit identified is the self-reinforcing bondage of Pro 5:22 and the structural consequence of Hab 1:4. This feeds into T5.7.
- `T2.8.3` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-2]** E — The evidence is not silent on deposit; T2.8.1–T2.8.2 identify and analyse it. This feeds directly into T5.7.
- `T2.8.3` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-3]** E — The evidence is not silent on deposit (T2.8.1–T2.8.2). This feeds directly into T5.7.

**T5.7 · Deposit Consequence**  (21)

- `T5.7.1` · cluster_finding · /cluster_synthesis — **[CLUSTER]** E — All six characteristics identified constitutional deposits (T2.8.1). The cluster's deposit-consequences map five distinct developmental trajectories: (a) worn-path habituation → default moral trajectory and progressive difficulty of departure (Chars 1 and 5); (b) constitutional transformation toward the evil-object's image → the person becoming what they practise (Char 4 — Hos 9:10); (c) conscience-silencing deposit → progressive incapacity for self-correction (Chars 3 and 5 — Pro 30:20; Jer 6:15); (d) will-hardening deposit → progressive foreclosure of the repentance option (Char 6 — Rev 16:9/11/21 — three successive judgments each deepening the hardening); (e) cognitive regression to instinct-level → rational comprehension bypassed by contempt (Char 6 — Jude 10). At cluster scope, the developmental consequence of all M10b deposits converges on a single outcome: progressive narrowing of the inner person's available moral space, until only divine intervention can reopen the possibility of departure.
- `T5.7.1` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-1]** E — T2.8 identified three deposits: (1) self-reinforcing bondage (Pro 5:22 — "held fast in the cords of his sin"); (2) structural perversion of justice (Hab 1:4); (3) social-moral contagion risk (Num 16:26). The developmental consequences of these deposits: (1) The self-reinforcing bondage deepens over time — the wicked person becomes progressively less accessible to correction (Isa 26:10 — "he does not learn righteousness" even under grace; Dan 12:10 — the eschatological incapacity to understand). The deposit of bondage produces the developmental consequence of progressive volitional foreclosure. (2) The structural perversion of justice corrupts the community's legal and moral environment, making it progressively harder for justice to function (Hab 1:4 — "the wicked surround the righteous; so justice goes forth perverted"). (3) The social contagion risk (Num 16:26) produces developmental community corruption if the warning to distance is not heeded.
- `T5.7.1` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-2]** E — T2.8 identified three deposits: (1) progressive deterioration (2Ti 3:13 — VCG-06); (2) expanding inner occupation (Mat 12:45 — VCG-06); (3) leaven-spreading through community (1Cor 5:8 — VCG-05). The developmental consequences: (1) The progressive-deterioration deposit produces escalating constitutional corruption — "from bad to worse" is not a stable state but an ongoing degradation trajectory. The deposit deepens with each stage, making each subsequent departure from the characteristic's grip more difficult without divine intervention. (2) The expanding-inner-occupation deposit produces a condition where the inner space becomes progressively more fully occupied by the constitutional evil — Mat 12:45 (VCG-06): the last state worse than the first shows the deposit accumulating toward maximum occupation. (3) The community-leaven deposit produces collective constitutional corruption spreading through communal relationships — 1Cor 5:8 (VCG-05) is the basis for the community's urgent response to expel the evil person (1Cor 5:13 — VCG-04).
- `T5.7.1` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-3]** E — T2.8 identified two primary deposits: (1) conscience-silencing (Jer 6:15; 8:12 — VCG-04); (2) accumulated shame-burden (Eze 44:13 — VCG-04); and a communal deposit (Ezr 9:11 — VCG-04). Developmental consequences: (1) The conscience-silencing deposit produces progressive incapacity for moral self-correction. Jer 6:15 and 8:12 (VCG-04) show the terminus: no shame, no blushing — the person has lost the inner faculty that would generate corrective response. Each subsequent act of abomination deepens the silencing deposit. (2) The shame-burden deposit (Eze 44:13 — VCG-04) produces permanent exclusion from sacred service — the abominations carried become a weight that bars relational access to the holy. (3) The communal-saturation deposit (Ezr 9:11 — VCG-04) produces a moral environment in which subsequent generations are formed within an abomination-saturated space, making reception of abominable norms the default rather than the exception.
- `T5.7.1` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-4]** E — T2.8 identified two deposits: (1) constitutional character-transformation (Hos 9:10 — VCG-04 — becoming detestable like the idol); (2) land-defilement as collective deposit (Jer 16:18 — VCG-04; Eze 33:29 — VCG-05). Developmental consequences: (1) The constitutional transformation deposit produces a self-reinforcing degradation trajectory: the person who has become detestable (Hos 9:10 — VCG-04) finds idolatrous abomination increasingly natural and the covenant orientation increasingly alien. This development can reach the Eze 8:17 (VCG-05) stage where abomination is treated as trivial — full developmental normalisation of what is abominable. (2) The land-defilement deposit produces an environmental legacy that shapes the formation of subsequent generations within a morally contaminated space (Ezr 9:11 — vc=66005 — VCG-04 parallel, from CHAR-3 corpus but describing CHAR-4's land-fill pattern).
- `T5.7.1` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-5]** E — T2.8 identified three deposits: (1) conscience-silencing (Pro 30:20 — VCG-04), (2) worn inner path (Job 22:15 — VCG-04), (3) iniquity's dominion-seeking (Psa 119:133 — VCG-04). Developmental consequences: (1) Conscience-silencing deposit: produces the incapacity for self-correction; the person who has reached Pro 30:20 stage ("I have done no wrong") can no longer generate the inner moral signal that would initiate departure from iniquity. Each subsequent commission deepens the silence. (2) Worn path deposit: produces a habitual moral trajectory that becomes the path of least inner resistance; the worn path is increasingly difficult to leave because it is the established inner groove of the character. (3) Dominion-seeking deposit: produces progressive constraint on volitional freedom — the will becomes increasingly bound to iniquity's direction, narrowing the range of genuine volitional choice available.
- `T5.7.1` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-6]** E — T2.8 identified a will-hardening deposit (Rev 16:9/11/21 — VCG-01: progressive hardening across three judgments). Developmental consequences: (1) The will-hardening deposit produces progressive incapacity for repentance — each iteration of blasphemous defiance under judgment deepens the deposit of hardness, narrowing the will's ability to orient toward repentance; at the terminus (Rev 16:21 — VCG-01), the deposit has produced a will that cannot bend even under catastrophic pressure. (2) The deposit produces cognitive regression — Jude 10 (vc=14355 — VCG-01): "destroyed by all that they, like unreasoning animals, understand instinctively" — the sustained contempt-as-substitute-for-understanding deposits a sub-rational cognitive posture that increasingly operates on instinct rather than rational moral comprehension.
- `T5.7.2` · cluster_finding · /cluster_synthesis — **[CLUSTER]** E — Generational consequence is evidenced in three of six characteristics. Char 5 (Job 21:19 — "God stores up their iniquity for their children"); Char 4 (Eze 20:4/30 — "abominations of their fathers"; Eze 20:30 — "go whoring after their detestable things" as generational replication; Jer 16:18 — land filled with abominations from preceding generations); Char 3 (Ezr 9:11 — communal abominations from preceding peoples filling the land). Chars 1, 2, and 6 do not directly evidence generational biological transmission, though Chars 1 and 2 evidence communal-environmental generational influence through culture and formation. The cluster's generational consequence evidence is concentrated in the abomination characteristics (Chars 3 and 4) and the iniquity-scheming characteristic (Char 5) — the three characteristics with the most developed environmental-deposit evidence.
- `T5.7.2` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-1]** E — Eze 18:20 (VCG-07) explicitly limits generational moral-accountability consequence: "the son shall not suffer for the iniquity of the father" — the characteristic's moral weight is personalised, not automatically transmitted. However, Num 16:26 (VCG-04) — the contagious-sweeping-away risk of proximity to the wicked in community — implies a communal-generational consequence if wickedness is institutionally embedded. The tension in the evidence is between personal moral accountability (Eze 18:20 — no automatic generational moral inheritance) and social-structural consequence (Hab 1:4; Num 16:26 — the wicked person's sustained operation damages the environment in which the next generation grows). The evidence therefore presents limited personal-moral generational consequence combined with real social-structural generational consequence.
- `T5.7.2` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-2]** E — Mat 12:39/16:4 (vc=14416/14441 — VCG-02) and Luk 11:29 (vc=14446 — VCG-02) address "this evil generation" as constitutionally evil — the generational framing implies a collective, trans-individual dimension of the characteristic. The evil generation is addressed as a unit, not merely as aggregated individuals, suggesting the constitutional evil operates at a generational-collective level. 2Ti 3:13 (vc=14451 — VCG-06) — "evil people and impostors will go on from bad to worse" — frames the trajectory as an ongoing generational pattern of teachers following teachers in decline. The evidence does not address biological/DNA generational transmission specifically; it addresses the socio-cultural and spiritual transmission of the characteristic across generations through community and teaching.
- `T5.7.2` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-3]** E — Yes. Ezr 9:11 (vc=66005 — VCG-04) specifically addresses generational communal consequence: "the land… is a land impure with the impurity of the peoples of the lands, with their abominations that have filled it from end to end with their uncleanness." The abominations of preceding peoples have saturated the land and become the environment into which Israel is entering — a generational communal deposit that preceded Israel's arrival and shaped the moral-spiritual environment they would inhabit. This is not direct biological transmission but moral-environmental generational consequence: the abominations of predecessors shape the environment of successors. The Mal 2:11 (vc=66004 — VCG-04) faithless mixed devotion also has generational implications (the children of the faithless union introducing foreign god-allegiance into the covenant community).
- `T5.7.2` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-4]** E — Yes, substantially. Eze 20:4 (vc=65994 — VCG-05): "let them know the abominations of their fathers" — the abominations of the fathers are a heritage requiring confrontation in the next generation. Eze 20:30 (vc=65912 — VCG-05): "Will you defile yourselves after the manner of your fathers and go whoring after their detestable things?" — the generational pattern of going after the fathers' detestable things is specifically named. Jer 16:18 (vc=65964 — VCG-04): the land filled with abominations is the environmental deposit of preceding generations that the current generation inhabits. The evidence is clear and direct about generational consequence: idolatrous abomination propagates across generations through the twin mechanisms of inherited patterns (Eze 20:30 — VCG-05) and environmental-land defilement (Jer 16:18 — VCG-04).
- `T5.7.2` · cluster_finding · /finding — **[CHAR-5]** E — Yes. Job 21:19 (vc=38209 — VCG-04): "God stores up their iniquity for their children" — explicit generational consequence is named: the accumulated iniquity of one generation is deferred to the next as an inherited burden. This is the primary generational-deposit evidence. The VCG-01 communal-trouble evidence (Psa 55:10 — vc=38239 — VCG-01: "iniquity and trouble are within it" — the city) shows that accumulated communal iniquity deposits a moral-environmental legacy that shapes subsequent inhabitants. The worn-path evidence (Job 22:15 — VCG-04: "the old way that wicked men have trod") implies a generational model: the path was established by predecessors and subsequent persons are warned not to follow it — the predecessors' iniquity has deposited a trajectory that the living must actively refuse.
- `T5.7.2` · cluster_finding · /silent — **[CHAR-6]** S — The 17-verse corpus does not directly evidence generational consequence for evil expressed through speech. The characteristic's deposit is individual and cosmic/eschatological in scope (VCG-01: the hardened individual will; the eschatological population under judgment) but not specifically described as generational in transmission. Rom 2:24 (vc=14357 — VCG-02): the moral failure of those who represent God causes God's name to be blasphemed "among the Gentiles" — this has a communal rather than strictly generational social impact, but it is not specifically a generational transfer. Generational consequence is not directly evidenced.
